debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G : Graph α β
x : α
h : (x : α) → @Graph.inessential α β G x
hx : x ∈ G.vertexSet
⊢ ¬Matroid.IsColoop (@Graph.matchingMatroid α β G) x

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
intro hcoloop
  have h₁ : G.inessential x := h x
  obtain ⟨M, hM, hM'⟩ := h₁
  have h₂ : x ∉ V(G, M) := hM'
  have h₃ : M ⊆ E(G) := by
    exact hM.edges_subset
  have h₄ : x ∈ V(G, M) := by
    by_contra h₄
    have h₅ : x ∈ V(G) := hx
    have h₆ : ∀ (y : α), y ∈ V(G, M) → y ∈ V(G) := by
      intro y hy
      exact (V_mk_subset)
    have h₇ : M ∈ matchingMatroid.indeps := by
      exact ⟨M, hM, by simp [h₃]⟩
    have h₈ : ¬(G.matchingMatroid.IsColoop x) := by
      -- This is a contradiction because if x were a coloop, then it would be in every basis.
      -- But we have a set V(G, M) that is in the independent sets and does not contain x.
      -- Therefore, x cannot be a coloop.
      simp_all [Matroid.IsColoop, Matroid.IsDependent, Disjoint]
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try tauto) <;>
      (try
        {
          aesop
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Set.subset_def, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
    exact h₈ hcoloop
  exact h₂ h₄
